coredata.dc:title: A portable and intelligent logic detector for simultaneous and in-situ detection of Al3+ and fluoride in groundwater
coredata.dc:creator: Che, Huachao
Tian, Xike
Wang, Jiahuan
Dai, Chu
Nie, Yulun
Li, Yong
Lu, Liqiang
coredata.dc:description: To develop a convenient and intelligent detector for simultaneous and in-situ detection of Al3+ and F- in groundwater, a novel organic probe called RBP has been prepared. With the increase of Al3+, RBP showed a significant fluorescence enhancement at 588 nm, and the detection limit was 0.130 mg/L. After combining with fluorescent internal standard CDs, the fluorescence of RBP-Al-CDs at 588 nm was quenched due to the replace of F- for Al3+, while the CDs at 460 nm remained unchanged, and the detection limit was 0.0186 mg/L. For convenient and intelligent detection, an RBP-based logic detector has been developed for simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F-. Within the ultra-trace, low concentration, and high concentration range of Al3+ and F-, the logic detector can achieve rapid feedback on their concentration levels (“U”, “L” and “H”) through different output modes of the signal lamps. The development of logical detector is of great significance for studying the in-situ chemical behavior of Al3+ and F- and for daily household detection.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Fluorescence detection
Organic molecular probe
Logic detector
Aluminum ion
Fluoride

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: The signal response of RBP towards Al3+
The signal response of RBP-Al-CDs towards F-
Characterization and spectral properties of RBP
Sensitive detection of Al3+ by RBP
Simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F- by logic detector
Detection mechanism towards Al3+ and F

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: a Synthesis process of RBP. 1b The FT IR spectrum of RBP. 1c UV absorption spectrum and fluorescence emission spectrum of RBP.

a and 2b are the selectivity and competitiveness diagrams of RBP for Al3+ detection, respectively. 2c Fluorescence spectra of RBP at different Al3+ concentrations, respectively. 2d Job's plot of RBP reacting with Al3+.

a Electric property and particle size change after mixing of RBP-Al and CDs, respectively. 3b The binding energy of Al in RBP-Al and RBP-Al-CDs. 3c Fluorescence spectra of RBP-Al-CDs at different F- concentrations, respectively. 3d The selectivity diagrams of RBP-Al-CDs for F- detection.

a Fluorescence visual diagram of RBP-Al-CDs solution with F- of different concentrations. 4b RGB values of fluorescence color of RBP-Al-CDs obtained through smartphone color software. 4c Linear relationship between RGB ratio and F- concentration.
a and 5b are the equivalent circuit diagram of logic detector for Al3+ and F-, respectively.

a The binding energy of O and N in RBP in the presence and absence of Al3+, respectively. 6b The schematic diagram of DFT calculation for LUMO and HOMO orbitals of RBH and pyridoxal hydrochloride, respectively. 6c Diagram of detection mechanism of Al3+ and F-. 6d and 6e are the TEM images of RBP-Al-CDs and RBP-Al-CDs+F-, respectively. 6f The EDS diagram of Al and F in RBP-Al-CDs+F-.
The truth tables for Al3+ detection.

head.ce:title: A portable and intelligent logic detector for simultaneous and in-situ detection of Al3+ and fluoride in groundwater

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Faculty of Materials Science and Chemistry, China University of Geosciences, Wuhan 430074, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Materials Science and Chemistry, China University of Geosciences

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Faculty of Materials Science and Chemistry, China University of Geosciences, Wuhan 430074, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Biogeology and Environmental Geology, China University of Geosciences, Wuhan 430074, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Biogeology and Environmental Geology, China University of Geosciences

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Key Laboratory of Biogeology and Environmental Geology, China University of Geosciences, Wuhan 430074, P.R. China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: Faculty of Materials Science and Chemistry, China University of Geosciences, Wuhan 430074, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Materials Science and Chemistry, China University of Geosciences

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: To develop a convenient and intelligent detector for simultaneous and in-situ detection of Al3+ and F- in groundwater, a novel organic probe called RBP has been prepared. With the increase of Al3+, RBP showed a significant fluorescence enhancement at 588 nm, and the detection limit was 0.130 mg/L. After combining with fluorescent internal standard CDs, the fluorescence of RBP-Al-CDs at 588 nm was quenched due to the replace of F- for Al3+, while the CDs at 460 nm remained unchanged, and the detection limit was 0.0186 mg/L. For convenient and intelligent detection, an RBP-based logic detector has been developed for simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F-. Within the ultra-trace, low concentration, and high concentration range of Al3+ and F-, the logic detector can achieve rapid feedback on their concentration levels (“U”, “L” and “H”) through different output modes of the signal lamps. The development of logical detector is of great significance for studying the in-situ chemical behavior of Al3+ and F- and for daily household detection.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Due to the dissolution of mineral elements in soil and the booming development of industries such as electroplating and chemical engineering, etc., excessive pollutants, especially Al3+ and F-, are invading groundwater and becoming one of the most serious and widely distributed pollutants in groundwater [1,2]. As an important source of drinking water, the content of Al3+ and F- in groundwater has become a key index to evaluate water quality, which is closely related to human health. Excessive intake of Al3+ will disturb the metabolism of phosphorus, and have adverse effects on the central nervous system, which is easy to cause Alzheimer's disease, while excessive F- is one of the causes of low human immunity, and even leukemia. In order to improve people's ability to prevent excessive Al3+ and F-, World Health Organization (WHO) have set the safe content limits of Al3+ and F- in drinking water as 0.3 mg/L and 1.5 mg/L, respectively [3]. In addition, a large number of techniques such as ICP, atomic fluorescence spectroscopy, ion chromatography, ion selective electrode, etc. have also been developed for sensitive and ultra-trace detection of Al3+ and F- [4]. However, the expensive instruments and high technical threshold seriously limit their wider use. Sampling preparation and analysis are also time consuming, limiting the capability of real-time detection and decision making. Recently, Bhamore et al. prepared a green CDs and successfully achieved the detection of Al3+ through chelation enhanced fluorescence mechanism, with a detection limit as low as 0.0025 μM. Lim et al. also successfully prepared probes for Al3+ and F- detection based on turn on/off of fluorescence, with detection limits of 1.89 × 10−8 M and 1.4 × 10−5 M, respectively [5–11]. These new methods based on colorimetry and fluorescence are easy to operate and low cost, they are still plagued by low sensitivity and poor accuracy. Particularly, traditional detection modes for a single target are not only inefficient, but also difficult to reveal the synergistic/antagonistic mechanisms of toxicity when different pollutants co-exist [12]. Therefore, it is of great significance to develop a convenient and intelligent detector for simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F-.

Electronic logic gates, originating from traditional computer microprocessors, have received widespread attention due to their convenience and intelligence, and are widely used in disease diagnosis, life analysis, and environmental monitoring [13–15]. Compared with traditional detectors, Logic detectors are developed based on the advantages of electronic logic gate and chemical probes. The researchers make use of the flexible signal input or output of logic gate circuit, and the logic establishment or not is defined as "1" and "0", which can realize the detection of target analytes and intelligent application of sensors in complex system [16,17]. Yang et al. constructed a binary Boolean NAND logic gate for rapid detection of nitrite [18]. In another work, L.X. Lu et al. designed an "and" logic gate for specific luminescence opening detection of Ag+ and SCN- [19]. It is foreseeable that a more sensitive input signal will facilitate the identification of logic detectors and greatly improve the detection accuracy [20]. Therefore, designing a detection unit that responds sensitively to Al3+ and F- is the key to building a logic detector.

Fluorescence analysis based on wavelength shift or intensity change of fluorescence to achieve qualitative and quantitative detection has always been known for its high sensitivity and signal visualization and so on [21–23]. With the vigorous development of fluorescence analysis methods, a large number of fluorescent materials such as Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs), Covalent Organic Frameworks (COFs), Carbon Quantum Dots (CDs), Gold Nanoparticle (AuNPs), etc. have emerged rapidly as bamboo shoots in spring, and are widely used in environmental monitoring and life analysis. Among them, fluorescent dyes, especially Rhodamine B derivatives, have attracted much attention because of their stable fluorescence and excellent anti-background interference, etc. [24,25]. Even so, Rhodamine B derivatives can only open the spiroamide structure to emit fluorescence in the presence of H+ or metal cations, etc., so it is difficult to be used for anion sensing [26]. Inspired by our previous studies, some metal ions bound to the probe can separate from the probe to form MnFx (Mn represents metal ion and X represents coordination number) due to their stronger binding ability to fluoride, leading to the fluorescence change [27–29]. Therefore, it is potential to construct a logic detector based on Rhodamine B derivative sensing unit to realize simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F-.

In this paper, Rhodamine B, hydrazine hydrate and pyridoxal hydrochloride were used as raw materials to prepare organic molecule RBP, In the presence of Al3+, the helical lactam structure of RBP was opened, turning on the fluorescence at 588 nm. Subsequently, with the addition of F-, the fluorescence was quenched due to the strong coordination ability of Al3+ and F-. In addition, the selectivity and competitiveness experiments of common interfering substances have also confirmed that there is no significant interference with the response signals of Al3+ and F-. Furthermore, a portable and intelligent logic detector based on RBP were fabricated for simultaneous and in-situ detection of Al3+ and F- in groundwater.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The signal response of RBP towards Al3+
ce:section.ce:para: RBP was prepared by using Rhodamine B, hydrazine hydrate and pyridoxal hydrochloride as raw materials (S1). For the sensitive detection of Al3+, the fluorescence spectra in the presence and absence of Al3+ were tested at different pH values, and the change of fluorescence intensity over time was studied at the most responsive pH parameters. Under the optimal parameters, gradually increase the concentration of Al3+ in 2 mL of RBP solution (0.2 mM), and then the fluorescence spectrum of the solution in the range of 380–750 nm was measured at an excitation wavelength of 360 nm (voltage: 400 V, slit width: 10 nm). The selective experiments of RBP (2 mL, 0.2 mM) for Al3+ were carried out by detecting of a series of metal ions, including Al3+, Fe3+, K+, Na+, Cu2+, Mg2+, Zn2+, Ba2+, Ca2+, Pb2+, Mn2+, Co2+, Cd2+ with same concentration, respectively, in which all technical operations and parameters (pH = 7.4; Excitation wavelength = 360 nm) remain consistent.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The signal response of RBP-Al-CDs towards F-
ce:section.ce:para: The complexation ratio of Al3+ to RBP was determined by Job curve. A sample solution with a total concentration of 0.2 mM was prepared by the selected metal ions and RBP, wherein the metal ion concentration ratio of each group varied from 0 to 0.9. Firstly, the maximum absorbance group was determined by the UV absorption spectrum of each solution measured by UV spectrophotometer, and then the complexation ratio of Al3+ and RBP was obtained by fitting the two groups of data.

RBP-Al-CDs was prepared under the optimal coordination ratio of RBP and Al3+ and the presence of fluorescent external standard CDs (S2), and its response sensitivity to F- was studied under different pH parameters. For the sensitive detection of F-, a series of F- solutions were added to Tris-HCl buffer solution (Optimal pH) of RBP-Al-CDs (2 mL, 10 μg/mL), and the fluorescence spectra were measured after mixing evenly, respectively. All data were measured three times in parallel. In addition, in order to explore the selectivity of RBP-Al-CDs for F- detection, 1 mM of Cl-, Br-, I-, HSO3 -, CO3 2-, HCO3 -, AC-, NO3 -and PO4 3- were added to the solution of RBP-Al-CDs in sequence, and their fluorescence spectra were tested under the same conditions, respectively. Similarly, in order to test the anti-interference ability of RBP-Al-CDs to fluoride detection, the above ions were added to the buffer solution of RBP-Al-CDs in the presence of fluoride (100 μM), and their fluorescence spectrum data were tested, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization and spectral properties of RBP
ce:section.ce:para: The RBH with lactam structure was synthesized by the reaction of rhodamine B and hydrazine hydrate, and then the RBP was obtained by reacting RBH with pyridoxal hydrochloride ( Fig. 1a). 1H NMR and 13C NMR were used to reveal the structural information of the intermediate RBH and the target RBP, and all characterization results showed that the synthesized product has a chemical structure consistent with the target molecule, and impurities and by-products have been removed (S3). In addition, the functional group information of RBP was analyzed by FT IR. As shown in Figure, the absorption peak at 3381 cm−1 was generated by the stretching vibration of -OH, 1734 cm−1 was attributed to the stretching vibration of CO, 1617 cm−1 was caused by the lactam structure, 1515 cm−1 corresponds to the CC stretching vibration of the aromatic ring, 1267 cm−1 was generated by the stretching vibration of C-O, and the strong absorption peak at 1218 cm−1 was generated by the stretching vibration of the saturated C-N on the benzene ring, 1119 cm−1 was caused by the stretching vibration of C-O-C (Fig. 1b). FT IR confirmed that RBP contained -OH, CO, CC, C-O-C and lactam structures, which were consistent with the results of 1H NMR and 13C NMR, confirmed that RBP has been successfully prepared [30]. Furthermore, due to the helical lactam structure of RBP, it has strong UV absorption at 230 nm, 280 nm and 360 nm, while the fluorescence performance of this non-ring-open state is not obvious (Fig. 1c) [31]. Under the optimal parameters, the fluorescence of RBP remained stable for a long time, which also confirmed the stability of molecular structure (S4).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensitive detection of Al3+ by RBP
ce:section.ce:para: The fluorescence of helical lactam structure in RBP can be turned on in the presence of some cations ( Fig. 2a), of which Al3+ has the most obvious fluorescence turning on effect. More importantly, in the presence of Al3+, even 100 times the concentration of other metal cations could not affect the fluorescence of RBP-Al (Fig. 2b), which confirmed that RBP-Al has a relatively stable structure. Although the fluorescence intensity of RBP increases with the decrease of pH because abundant H+ can open the helical lactam structure of it, the difference of fluorescence intensity between RBP and RBP-Al was the largest at pH= 7.4 (The response signal is the most sensitive) (S5). Under the selected parameters, with the increase of Al3+, the fluorescence of RBP gradually increases at 588 nm, and the detection limit for Al3+ was 0.130 mg/L (Fig. 2c), which is lower than that reported in many literatures (S13) [32]. The complexation ratio of Al3+ to RBP was determined by Job curve. A sample solution with a total concentration of 0.2 mM was prepared by Al3+ and RBP, wherein the metal ion concentration ratio of each group varied from 0 to 0.9. As the concentration proportion of Al3+ gradually increases, the fluorescence intensity of the solution also gradually increases, reaching the maximum at the proportion of 0.32 (Fig. 2d). Therefore, the saturated complexation ratio of RBP and Al3+ is 2:1 according to the amount of material, and the result was also confirmed by ESI-HRMS (S7).

After the coordination of RBP and Al3+ at 2:1, the fluorescence of RBP-Al can be significantly quenched by F- due to the competition of F- for Al3+ (S6). Considering the stable structure and strong positive charge of RBP-Al, increasing the negative charge of the fluorescent internal standard not only significantly improves signal visibility and sensitivity, but also benefits its anti-interference ability. Therefore, a CDs with abundant amino and carboxyl groups and average particle size of 4.2 nm was prepared as internal fluorescent standard for F- detection (S8). After the mixing of RBP-Al (negative charge) and CDs (positive charge), they are tightly combined due to electrostatic effect, and the hydrated particle size also increases ( Fig. 3a). Furthermore, the infrared absorption peak frequency attributed to Al-O in RBP-Al increases (From 503 cm−1 to 472 cm−1) after the addition of CDs, while the characteristic double absorption peak of amino groups (700 cm−1 and 669 cm−1) of CDs in RBP-Al-CDs appears (S9). XPS also showed that the binding energy of Al increases from 73 eV to 73.3 eV after RBP-Al combined with CDs, indicating that the hydroxyl group of CDs and the Al3+ site of RBP-Al had formed a stable chemical bond, and RBP-Al-CDs had been successfully constructed (Fig. 3b) [33].

In order to reveal the detection performance of RBP-Al-CDs for fluoride under different pH conditions, the ratio fluorescence intensity (I588/I460) of RBP-Al-CDs solution was studied in water samples with and without F-. As shown in S10, when pH was less than 7.4, because the protonation of CDs enhances the fluorescence at 460 nm, I588/I460 gradually decreases with the decrease of pH. In the presence of F-, although the substitution of fluoride for Al3+ will lead to fluorescence decrease of RBP, high concentration of H+ can still partially turn on RBP fluorescence, and I588/I460 gradually increases with the decrease of pH. When pH> 8.0, because Al3+ was precipitated, RBP fluorescence rapidly decreased to stable, and I588/I460 decreased firstly and then remained stable with the increase of pH. When pH = 7.4, the fluorescence difference between RBP-Al-CDs and RBP-Al-CDs + F- was the most obvious, which confirms that the detection of F- under this condition has the highest sensitivity. In addition, when pH = 7.4, I588/I460 could remain stable for at least 90 min, which can meet the smooth completion of the detection (S11). Therefore, in all subsequent experiments, the reaction was carried out in Tris-HCl buffer solution with pH= 7.4.

By introducing stable fluorescent internal standard CDs, a dual emission fluorescent probe RBP-Al-CDs was constructed for visual sensing of F-. As shown in Fig. 3c, under the optimal detection conditions (pH=7.4), with the increase of F-, the fluorescence of RBP (588 nm) gradually quenches while the CDs (460 nm) basically remains unchanged, and the fluorescence color of the solution showed obvious orange to light blue changes. The LOD of F- was calculated as 0.0186 mg/L and lower than many existing reports (S11 and S12) [34]. In order to verify the selectivity of RBP-Al-CDs for F- detection, Cl-, Br-, I-, HSO3 -, CO3 2-, HCO3 -, Ac-, NO3 -, PO4 3- and F- were successively added to RBP-Al-CDs solution and measured the fluorescence spectra, respectively. As shown in Fig. 3d, although CO3 2- and PO4 3- could precipitate Al3+, resulting in the fluorescence of RBP decreased slightly, the effect was negligible compared with F-. When the above ions are added to RBP-Al-CDs with F-, the fluorescence will not change significantly, which proves that RBP-Al-CDs has strong anti-interference ability for F- detection (S14). In addition, the spiking and recovery experiments in actual water samples show that the recovery rate of Al3+ and F- were stable at 90.79 %− 104.2 % and 90.1 %− 113.7 %, respectively, which confirms its practicability in detecting F- in complex environments (S15) [35,36].

In order to easily read the detection signal of F-, the fluorescence chroma value of RBP-Al-CDs in the presence of F- was obtained by smart phone. Under the irradiation of 365 nm UV lamp, the fluorescence color of RBP-Al-CDs changes from orange to blue with the increase of F- (0–100 μM) ( Fig. 4a). Accordingly, the corresponding chroma values (RGB) of RBP-Al-CDs under different F- concentrations can be read by the color recognition software of smart phones, respectively [37]. As shown in Fig. 4b, with the increase of F-, the G and B values of RBP-Al-CDs gradually increase, while the R value does not change significantly. The G/R and B/R values show a good linear relationship with F- concentration in the range of 0–100 μM, and the linear equations were Y1 = 0.531 + 0.008 ×C1 (R2 = 0.993) and Y2 = 0.433 + 0.010 × C2 (R2 = 0.997), respectively (Fig. 4c). Therefore, the fluorescence chroma of RBP-Al-CDs under different F- concentrations can be read by smart phone, and then the rapid, convenient detection of F- can be realized.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F- by logic detector
ce:section.ce:para: Considering the urgent demand for detection of Al3+ and F- in the field and home, the development of reliable, convenient and intelligent logic detector has attracted much attention. Considering that the LOD of RBP for Al3+ is 0.130 mg/L, while the safety limit is 0.30 mg/L (Safe concentration recommended by WHO). Under the excitation of UV light with fixed wavelength (360 nm), the fluorescence intensity of RBP under different concentrations of Al3+ was used as the input signal, and the signal light of the logic detector was taken as the corresponding output signal. As shown in Fig. 5a, Gate a1 was used to reflect whether the content of Al3+ in the environment reaches the LOD (0.130 mg/L) of RBP. When CAl 3+ <LOD (0.13 mg/L), the signal input is "0" and there is no signal light output ("0"), indicating ultra trace amounts of Al3+ (U). When LOD ≤ CAl 3+ ≤ Safety limit (0.3 mg/L), the output signal of Gate a2 is "1", which indicated that the concentration of Al3+ is low (L) within the safe range. Only when CAl 3+ >0.3 mg/L, gate a2 output signal is "1", corresponding to high concentration of Al3+ (H) ( Table 1). Compared to the detection of Al3+, the logic signal output of F- adopts a more accurate and convenient mode. When the excitation wavelength and the amount of Al3+ (RBP: Al3+ = 2：1) are fixed, the concentration of F- is used as the input signal, and the output signal is the corresponding RGB value of fluorescence. Combined with the linear equations between F- and G/R (or B/R), the concentration of F- in the range of 0–100 µM can be achieved (Fig. 5b). The signal truth value of logic detector for F- is similar to that for Al3+, only replacing CAl 3+ with the corresponding CF -. In addition, the cell survival rate of T75 was 93.6 %, which confirmed that RBP had low biotoxicity and has the potential to be used for the detection of Al3+ and F- in cells.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection mechanism towards Al3+ and F
ce:section.ce:para: Considering that CDs only acts as an internal standard and does not involve the change of fluorescence intensity or emission wavelength, it is only necessary to determine the fluorescence response mechanism of RBP and RBP-Al to Al3+ and F-, respectively. When Al3+ was added to RBP, oxygen atom of RBP was easy to combine with Al3+ to form RBP-Al, and the electron binding energy of O decreases significantly from 531.9 eV to 531.7 eV ( Fig. 6a). The HOMO orbital energy level of RBH is between the HOMO and LUMO energy levels of pyridoxal hydrochloride. RBH with electron giving ability can transfer electrons to the HOMO orbital of pyridoxal hydrochloride, which makes the electrons originally in the LUMO orbital of pyridoxal hydrochloride unable to jump to its HOMO orbital, so there is no fluorescence emission process of excited electrons returning to the ground state. Therefore, the RBH in the RBP molecule, as the receptor part, is connected with the fluorophore of pyridoxal hydrochloride through the spacer, and RBP does not have fluorescence due to the photoinduced electron transfer (PET) effect [38,39]. In the presence of Al3+, the lactam screw loop structure of RBP molecule opens, and the PET between the receptor and the fluorophore is closed, making the RBP emit fluorescence (Fig. 6b). When F- was added to RBP-Al-CDs, a new rod-like substance consistent with AlF3 appeared in the system, accompanied by a decrease in RBP fluorescence intensity. Considering the obvious aggregation of F- and Al3+ (Fig. 6c-f), it is confirmed that Al3+ can be snatched from RBP-Al molecules to form AlF3 and make RBP return to the closed loop state. After 5 cycles, the detection performance of fluoride remains above 90 % (S15).

Convenient and intelligent detection of Al3+ and F- in the field and at home is highly anticipated. In this work, RBP was developed for simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F-, and the detection limit were 0.13 mg/L and 0.98 μM, respectively. In addition, an RBP-based logic detector has been developed for simultaneous detection of Al3+ and F-. Within the ultra-trace (below detection limit), low concentration (safety concentration), and high concentration (exceeding safety limit) range of Al3+ and F-, the logic detector can achieve rapid feedback on their concentration levels (“U”, “L” and “H”) through different output modes of the signal lamps.

Due to the natural release of soil, rocks, and minerals and the improper discharge of electrolytic and chemical industries, excessive Al3+ and F- enter the groundwater, bringing huge hidden dangers to the ecosystem and health. Moreover, the strong complexing ability of F- and multiple metal ions, and the pH sensitivity of Al3+, their content is directly related to water quality parameters such as pH, hardness etc., which can be used as an important indicator of water quality change trend. Therefore, developing convenient and intelligent devices to simultaneously and in situ detect Al3+ and F- is of great significance.

Huachao Che: Data curation, Writing-original draft. Xike Tian: Formal analysis, Resources. Jiahuan Wang: Conceptualization. Chu Dai: Project administration. Yulun Nie: Supervision, Funding acquisition. Yong Li: Investigation. Liqiang Lu: Project administration.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 42107085, 51878633), the “Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities”, the Postdoctoral Science Foundation of China (No. 2021M703006) and Postdoctoral Innovation Research Foundation of Hubei Province of China.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.jhazmat.2023.131956.

Supplementary material
.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Highly selective colorimetric/fluorometric dual-channel fluoride ion probe, and its capability of differentiating cancer cells
Appl Mater Interfaces

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Facile green synthesis of carbon dots from Pyrus pyrifolia fruit for assaying of Al3+ ion via chelation enhanced fluorescence mechanism
J Mol Liq
